name: migrations
context: Supabase database migrations — create tables, manage schema changes, seed data, and sync with remote
info: Supabase migrations are timestamped SQL files in `supabase/migrations/` that define your database schema. The CLI manages the migration lifecycle: `supabase migration new` creates a blank file, `supabase db diff` auto-generates SQL from schema changes made in Studio, `supabase db push` applies pending migrations to the remote database, `supabase db pull` pulls remote schema into local migrations, and `supabase db reset` drops and recreates the local database from migrations + seed.sql. Migrations run in order by timestamp. They are idempotent — each runs exactly once, tracked in the `supabase_migrations.schema_migrations` table. Use migrations for all schema changes: tables, columns, indexes, RLS policies, functions, triggers, and extensions. Never modify production schemas manually.
body:
# Supabase Migrations

Migrations are timestamped SQL files that define your database schema. They're the source of truth for your Postgres schema.

## Creating Migrations

### Manual (blank file)
```bash
supabase migration new create_posts_table
# Creates: supabase/migrations/20260411120000_create_posts_table.sql
```

Then write the SQL:
```sql
-- supabase/migrations/20260411120000_create_posts_table.sql

CREATE TABLE posts (
  id uuid PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  user_id uuid REFERENCES auth.users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE NOT NULL,
  title text NOT NULL,
  body text,
  published boolean DEFAULT false,
  created_at timestamptz DEFAULT now() NOT NULL,
  updated_at timestamptz DEFAULT now() NOT NULL
);

-- Enable RLS
ALTER TABLE posts ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;

-- Policies
CREATE POLICY "Users can read own posts"
  ON posts FOR SELECT
  USING (auth.uid() = user_id);

CREATE POLICY "Users can insert own posts"
  ON posts FOR INSERT
  WITH CHECK (auth.uid() = user_id);

CREATE POLICY "Users can update own posts"
  ON posts FOR UPDATE
  USING (auth.uid() = user_id)
  WITH CHECK (auth.uid() = user_id);

CREATE POLICY "Users can delete own posts"
  ON posts FOR DELETE
  USING (auth.uid() = user_id);

-- Public read for published posts
CREATE POLICY "Anyone can read published posts"
  ON posts FOR SELECT
  USING (published = true);

-- Indexes
CREATE INDEX idx_posts_user_id ON posts(user_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_posts_created_at ON posts(created_at DESC);

-- Updated_at trigger
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION update_updated_at()
RETURNS TRIGGER AS $$
BEGIN
  NEW.updated_at = now();
  RETURN NEW;
END;
$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;

CREATE TRIGGER posts_updated_at
  BEFORE UPDATE ON posts
  FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE FUNCTION update_updated_at();
```

### Auto-generated (diff from Studio changes)
```bash
# Make changes in Studio (http://localhost:54323)
# Then generate migration from the diff
supabase db diff --use-migra -f add_comments_table
```

## Applying Migrations

```bash
# Apply to remote database
supabase db push

# Apply to local database (reset and re-run all)
supabase db reset
```

## Pulling Remote Schema

```bash
# Pull remote schema into a new migration
supabase db pull
# Creates a migration file with the current remote schema
```

## Seeding Data

Create `supabase/seed.sql` for test data:

```sql
-- supabase/seed.sql

-- Insert test user (only works locally with service role)
INSERT INTO auth.users (id, email)
VALUES ('d0fc4c64-a3d6-4f27-a427-1bca77f5b768', 'test@example.com');

-- Insert test posts
INSERT INTO posts (user_id, title, body, published)
VALUES
  ('d0fc4c64-a3d6-4f27-a427-1bca77f5b768', 'First Post', 'Hello world!', true),
  ('d0fc4c64-a3d6-4f27-a427-1bca77f5b768', 'Draft Post', 'Work in progress', false);
```

Seed runs automatically after migrations during `supabase db reset`.

## Common Table Patterns

### User profiles (extends auth.users)
```sql
CREATE TABLE profiles (
  id uuid PRIMARY KEY REFERENCES auth.users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  full_name text,
  avatar_url text,
  bio text,
  created_at timestamptz DEFAULT now() NOT NULL
);

-- Auto-create profile on signup
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION create_profile_on_signup()
RETURNS TRIGGER AS $$
BEGIN
  INSERT INTO public.profiles (id, full_name)
  VALUES (NEW.id, NEW.raw_user_meta_data ->> 'full_name');
  RETURN NEW;
END;
$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql SECURITY DEFINER;

CREATE TRIGGER on_auth_user_created
  AFTER INSERT ON auth.users
  FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE FUNCTION create_profile_on_signup();
```

### Many-to-many (tags)
```sql
CREATE TABLE tags (
  id uuid PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  name text UNIQUE NOT NULL
);

CREATE TABLE post_tags (
  post_id uuid REFERENCES posts(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  tag_id uuid REFERENCES tags(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  PRIMARY KEY (post_id, tag_id)
);
```

### Soft delete
```sql
ALTER TABLE posts ADD COLUMN deleted_at timestamptz;

-- RLS policy to hide soft-deleted rows
CREATE POLICY "Hide soft-deleted posts"
  ON posts FOR SELECT
  USING (deleted_at IS NULL AND auth.uid() = user_id);
```

## Migration Best Practices

1. **One concern per migration** — don't mix table creation with data backfill
2. **Include RLS policies** with table creation — don't leave tables unprotected
3. **Add indexes** on foreign keys and columns used in RLS policies
4. **Use `gen_random_uuid()`** for primary keys (built into Postgres 14+)
5. **Reference `auth.users(id)`** for user-owned tables with `ON DELETE CASCADE`
6. **Never edit applied migrations** — create a new migration for changes
7. **Test with `supabase db reset`** before pushing to production

## Environment Variables

| Variable | Purpose |
|----------|----------|
| `SUPABASE_DB_URL` | Direct Postgres connection for remote operations |
| `SUPABASE_ACCESS_TOKEN` | CLI authentication for `supabase db push` |

sections.title: Creating migrations
sections.body: `supabase migration new <name>` creates a timestamped SQL file. Write DDL statements: CREATE TABLE, ALTER TABLE, CREATE INDEX, CREATE POLICY, CREATE FUNCTION. For auto-generation, make changes in Studio then run `supabase db diff --use-migra -f <name>` to capture the diff as a migration.

sections.title: Applying and syncing
sections.body: `supabase db push` applies pending migrations to the linked remote project. `supabase db reset` drops and recreates the local database from all migrations + seed.sql. `supabase db pull` generates a migration from the remote schema. Migrations are tracked in `supabase_migrations.schema_migrations` — each runs exactly once.

sections.title: Seeding data
sections.body: Create `supabase/seed.sql` with INSERT statements for test data. Seed runs after all migrations during `supabase db reset`. You can insert into `auth.users` for test accounts (only locally). Seed data should be idempotent — use `ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING` or check existence.

sections.title: Common schema patterns
sections.body: **User profiles**: table with `id uuid REFERENCES auth.users(id)` + trigger to auto-create on signup. **Timestamps**: `created_at timestamptz DEFAULT now()` + trigger for `updated_at`. **Soft delete**: `deleted_at timestamptz` column with RLS policy filtering nulls. **Many-to-many**: junction table with composite primary key. **Enums**: `CREATE TYPE status AS ENUM ('draft', 'published', 'archived')`.

links.context: Include RLS policies in migration files
Queries operate on the schema defined by migrations

related.note: CLI is the primary tool for running migrations
RLS policies should be included in migration files
Prisma Migrate is an alternative that generates SQL from a schema file

scripts.name: Create table migration
scripts.description: Generate a migration file with a table definition, RLS, and indexes

filePath.description: Path to the created migration file

depends.reason: CLI is required for migration commands

author: dojo-community
